mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.0 percent, labor market slack signals severe demand destruction and recessionary pressures, causing investors to rapidly de-risk and producing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 8.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand recovery, driving corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day forward returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices indicate strengthening manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which translates into higher corporate profit margins and positive equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of observations during mid-cycle expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect immediate economic momentum and rising wage expenditures, prompting short-term equity inflows and positive S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS revisions show zero predictive power for 5-day forward returns (Rank IC consistently below 0.01 across all rolling windows).

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 230.0, inflation expectations accelerate and force monetary authorities to tighten policy, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 230.0 are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in over 50% of cases, negating the inflation penalty effect.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and real personal consumption expenditures validates sustainable aggregate demand expansion, reducing recession risk premiums and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal from GDPC1 and RSAFS yields a negative or zero Rank IC for 20-day forward returns in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, further increases in industrial production signal capacity constraints and rising input costs, which erode corporate margins and trigger negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during late-cycle expansions produce positive 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances, contradicting the capacity constraint thesis.
